name: RSS to LinkedIn & Instagram AI Publisher with Auto Image Generation

nodes.name: Watch RSS Feed for New Articles

parameters.text:
=You are a social media strategist.

Create platform-ready social media content from this RSS article.

Article Title:
{{$json.article_title}}

Article Description:
{{$json.article_description}}

Article Link:
{{$json.article_link}}

Generate the output in valid JSON only.

Rules:
- LinkedIn post should be professional, insightful, and include the article link.
- Instagram caption should be short, engaging, and include hashtags.
- Instagram image prompt should describe a clean, modern, scroll-stopping visual.
- Do not use markdown.
- Do not add fake facts.
- Keep the tone clear and human.

- Do not mention "we", "our", or "us" unless the article is from our own company.
- LinkedIn post should be under 900 characters.
- Instagram caption should be under 300 characters.
- Hashtags should be relevant and maximum 5.
- Do not generate fake statistics.
- Avoid copyrighted logo descriptions unless the article clearly mentions the brands.
- Return valid JSON only, no explanation.

Return this exact JSON structure:

{
  "content_angle": "",
  "linkedin_post": "",
  "instagram_caption": "",
  "instagram_image_prompt": "",
  "hashtags": []
}

parameters.content:
## Step 1: Watch RSS Feed for New Articles

### What this node does
This node monitors an RSS feed and checks for new articles every hour.

### How it works
Whenever a new item appears in the RSS feed, this node triggers the workflow automatically.  
It receives article data such as the title, link, publish date, content snippet, and description from the RSS feed.

## Step 2: Extract Article Data

### What this node does
This node cleans and organizes the raw RSS feed data into simple fields that can be used in the next steps.

### How it works
It extracts the main article information from the RSS output:

- article_title
- article_link
- article_description
- published_date

## Step 3: Generate Social Media Content

### What this node does
This node uses AI to create social media content from the RSS article.

### How it works
It sends the article title, description, and link to the AI model and asks it to generate structured JSON output containing:

- content_angle
- linkedin_post
- instagram_caption
- instagram_image_prompt
- hashtags

## Step 4: Parse AI JSON Output

### What this node does
This node converts the AI-generated JSON text into clean workflow fields.

### Why it is important
AI sometimes returns JSON as plain text.  
This node makes sure the output becomes proper structured data that can be used by the image generation, LinkedIn, and Instagram nodes.

## Step 5: Generate an Image

### What this node does
This node generates an image for the social media post.

### How it works
It uses Google Gemini image generation and takes the `instagram_image_prompt` created by the AI content node.  
The prompt describes the visual style, layout, and concept for the image.

# Step 7: Upload a File

### What this node does
This node uploads the generated image and converts it into a public image URL.

### How it works
The image generated by Gemini is passed as a file/binary output to this node.  
UploadToURL uploads the image and returns a public CDN URL.

### Why it is important
Instagram and many social platforms require a public image URL for posting.  
This node solves that problem by converting the generated image file into a shareable hosted URL.

## Step 6: Publish LinkedIn Post with Image

### What this node does
This node publishes the AI-generated LinkedIn post along with the generated image.

### How it works
It takes the LinkedIn post text from the **Parse AI JSON Output** node:

## Node 8: Publish Instagram Post

### What this node does
This node publishes the generated image and caption to Instagram.

### How it works
It takes the public image URL from the UploadToURL node and uses the AI-generated Instagram caption from the parsed AI output.

# Workflow Overview

### This workflow automatically monitors an RSS feed for new articles and converts each article into ready-to-publish social media content.  
### It extracts the article title, link, description, and publish date, then uses AI to generate a LinkedIn post, Instagram caption, hashtags, and an image prompt.  
### The workflow creates an AI-generated image, uploads it to a public URL using UploadToURL, and publishes the final content to LinkedIn and Instagram.
